example.error: the requested transition conflicts with current state or revision

Conflict.description: The requested transition conflicts with current state or revision.

example.error: the principal, origin, content type, or csrf proof is not authorized

Forbidden.description: The principal, origin, content type, or CSRF proof is not authorized.

example.error: the request failed without exposing internal details

InternalError.description: The request failed without exposing internal details.

example.error: the bounded endpoint is temporarily rate limited

RateLimited.description: The bounded endpoint is temporarily rate limited.

example.error: a required dependency or bounded worker is temporarily unavailable

Unavailable.description: A required dependency or bounded worker is temporarily unavailable.

error.description: Generic, non-secret error message suitable for an operator.

agentBearer.description: Device-scoped managed-agent credential. Never paste it into the interactive browser reference.

cookieSession.description: Hardened browser session. HTTPS deployments use __Host-mesh_session. Unsafe methods also require the matching CSRF cookie value in X-Mesh-CSRF plus the exact configured Origin.
cookieSession.name: mesh_session

legacyAdminBearer.description: Optional deployment administrator credential. Disabled unless explicitly configured; do not place it in a browser or persist it in documentation tooling.

info.description: The canonical contract for Mesh browser, desktop, administrator, installer, and managed-agent endpoints. Browser sessions use the mesh_session cookie (or __Host-mesh_session over HTTPS). Unsafe cookie-authenticated requests must send the readable mesh_csrf or __Host-mesh_csrf cookie value in X-Mesh-CSRF and must originate from the configured exact public origin. A desktop sign-in response returns its high-entropy poll secret once and uses Cache-Control: no-store. Keep the secret in the native client. Never place it in the verification URL, logs, or documentation tools. Configured legacy administrator bearer authentication is CSRF-exempt. Managed-agent bearer credentials are device-scoped. Examples contain placeholders only; never paste production credentials into documentation tools.
info.title: Mesh control-plane API

get.description: Returns the node certificate and complete signed configuration bundle for an authenticated agent.

200.description: Current certificate and signed configuration bundle.

post.description: Signs the supplied node public key when renewal policy and certificate-generation gates permit it.

get.description: Returns the node-specific signed desired artifact. If-None-Match may use the prior signature and receive 304.

304.description: The supplied configuration signature is current.

post.description: Records a bounded failure reason for the exact desired configuration revision.
Replaces the active agent credential hash while preserving one bounded overlap for recovery from response loss.
Reports the exact configuration, certificate, credential, process, resolver, and probe evidence observed by the managed agent.
Consumes a one-use recovery token and returns a replacement agent credential binding and current signed bundle.
Publishes allowlisted aggregate observations bound to the latest accepted heartbeat sequence.

get.description: Returns the newest control and identity events with actor attribution and no secret material.

post.description: Consumes one recovery code after same-origin and JSON-content checks and creates a short-lived browser session.

Uses the request ID and one-time poll secret to read the request state. Pending, denied, and expired responses contain no credentials. The first completion after approval creates a normal Mesh session and returns its session and CSRF cookies.

200.description: Current desktop authorization state and, once, an authorized session.

example.error: the desktop client polled before the server-provided interval

429.description: The desktop client polled before the server-provided interval.

Retry-After.description: Positive delta-seconds before the client may poll again.

post.description: Creates a desktop sign-in request that expires after five minutes in the configured shared identity store. Any current control-plane replica can approve or complete it. The response returns the poll secret once. The store keeps only its hash, and the native client must never put the secret in a URL or browser.

Approves an outstanding request for the principal in the current browser session, or denies the request. Legacy bearer authentication cannot make this decision. Approval requires the normal browser cookies, matching CSRF proof, and exact configured Origin.

get.description: Returns deployment-specific availability for OIDC, legacy browser login, and break-glass login.
Consumes the provider callback and transaction cookie, creates the Mesh browser session, and redirects to the bound return path.

parameters.description: Opaque OIDC state bound to the transaction cookie.
parameters.name: state

parameters.description: Provider authorization code. Mutually exclusive with error.
parameters.name: code

parameters.description: Provider error code. Mutually exclusive with code.
parameters.name: error

post.description: Creates a bounded OIDC transaction after same-origin and JSON-content checks. The browser must follow the returned provider URL.

get.description: Returns non-secret code identifiers, expiry, state, and the configured usable-code floor.

post.description: Stores only the verifier for a newly generated one-use recovery code. A recovery-authenticated principal cannot register replacements.

delete.description: Permanently revokes the selected unused recovery code.

post.description: Consumes a one-time enrollment token, signs the node-generated Nebula public key, and binds the agent credential hash.

200.description: Initial certificate and signed configuration bundle.

post.description: Validates a one-time enrollment token and returns the public network and node plan needed by the installer.
post.summary: Inspect an enrollment before key generation

get.description: Returns one authoritative fleet snapshot across every network.
Returns a secret-free fleet projection separate from lifecycle health.
Returns the configured public release-bundle and bootstrap-handoff locations without exposing credentials.
Returns the non-secret network inventory.

post.description: Creates an encrypted Nebula certificate authority and configuration-signing identity for one non-overlapping overlay CIDR. listen_port defaults to 4242 and certificate_ttl_hours defaults to 8760 when omitted.

get.description: Returns the staged CA transition, node convergence, and currently available actions.

post.description: Performs one revision-bound prepare, activate, finalize, complete, or abort transition.

get.description: Returns the desired DNS and optional native resolver state plus active resolver projection.

put.description: Updates DNS and optional native resolver settings under an optimistic network revision.

get.description: Returns the desired managed Nebula firewall policy and its revision metadata.

put.description: Atomically replaces the managed policy under an optimistic network revision.

get.description: Returns the active policy, staged target, canaries, convergence, and available rollout actions.

post.description: Starts, pauses, resumes, promotes, or rolls back a revision-bound canary rollout.

put.description: Compiles candidate rules for every active node without changing signed state.

get.description: Returns lifecycle, redundancy, rollout, and revocation health for one network.
Returns pending, active, and revoked node inventory for one network.

post.description: Reserves one overlay identity and creates a one-time enrollment credential.

201.description: Created pending node and one-time enrollment material.

get.description: Returns exact control and optional runtime evidence for the network deployment checklist.
Returns selected relay nodes and the active relay projection.

put.description: Selects up to eight pending or active relay nodes under an optimistic network revision.

post.description: Irreversibly removes the network authority and credentials while permanently reserving its name and CIDR.

get.description: Returns per-prefix gateway weights, MTU, metric, and convergence.

post.description: Updates owner weights and route attributes for one exact managed prefix.

get.description: Returns the current certificate-first route ownership transfer and convergence evidence.

post.description: Stages exact prefixes from one active owner to another while preserving the current route.
Promotes or completes the active transfer only after its certificate convergence gate.
Cancels the active transfer, waiting for certificate cleanup when issuance has already occurred.
Creates a bounded one-time recovery credential for an active identity whose private key remains trusted.
Removes expired issuance and blocklist history only after the complete certificate authority plus safety margin has ended.
Issues a same-key replacement, blocklists the old certificate through expiry, and returns an idempotent receipt.
Atomically removes a never-enrolled node and releases its reservations.
Invalidates prior enrollment tokens and returns one replacement for a still-pending node.

put.description: Issues a same-key certificate with the new groups and blocklists the previous certificate.

post.description: Revokes the old identity and creates one pending replacement carrying its lifecycle metadata.
Performs an exact-name-, revision-, and idempotency-bound trust cutoff, invalidates credentials, and blocklists every applicable certificate.
Immediately revokes the node but lacks the explicit confirmation and idempotent receipt contract. New clients should use /revocation.
post.summary: Revoke a node using the legacy endpoint

get.description: Returns the certificate-first routed-subnet membership edit for one node.

post.description: Stages an exact desired routed-subnet set and waits for certificate convergence.
Commits or completes the edit after its exact certificate convergence gate.
Cancels the edit, waiting for certificate cleanup when needed.

put.description: Updates site, failure-domain, and public endpoint metadata without changing certificate identity.

delete.description: Revokes the active cookie session and clears both session cookies. Legacy bearer requests cannot use this endpoint.

get.description: Returns the authenticated principal, role, permissions, and session lifetime when applicable.

post.description: Exchanges the deployment administrator token for hardened session and CSRF cookies. OIDC is preferred where configured.

get.description: Lists active web and desktop sessions, optionally including revoked sessions.

parameters.description: Maximum number of sessions, from 1 through 256.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Include revoked and expired session records.
parameters.name: include_revoked

delete.description: Revokes the selected session idempotently and clears cookies when the caller revokes its own session.

get.description: Returns success when the HTTP process is running. This endpoint does not prove durable-store readiness.
Checks the configured durable-store dependency and returns either ready or unavailable.

head.description: Returns the same status code as GET /readyz without a response body.

servers.description: The same Mesh control-plane origin that served this contract.
